summary:
I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Organization & Talent Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact.
Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve people experience—through human-centered, data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: Facilitate CEO/HR discovery {cross|interviews} to diagnose absenteeism and engagement drivers; analyze leave data to quantify costs
Upskill an HR team to audit 300+ leave cases and present executive insights on $362K in costs, driving {od|policy redesigns} and training
Run focus groups and 1:1 interviews with an NGO leadership team; analyze qualitative {data|data} to surface {od|accountability gaps} and redesign processes
Deliver {ld|onboarding and training initiatives} for 70+ staff, streamlining {cross|cross-functional} processes and reducing onboarding time by 75%
Facilitate {ld|listening sessions and needs assessments} with 25 global {cross|stakeholders}, producing 3 new {ld|learning programs} aligned to needs
Manage 20+ programs and 10+ vendors, refining program positioning and {cross|communications strategy} via {data|feedback analysis}
Facilitate {ld|training webinars} for 600+ participants, boosting {ex|program engagement} and achieving a 78% {ex|program enrollment rate}
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to amplify employee voice and guide C-suite decisions on {data|engagement and satisfaction drivers}
Led a {ld|manager effectiveness initiative}, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed {ex|engagement strategies}
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and {perf|performance review}
Improved employee experience via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects focusing on {ex|survey development, rewards & recognition}, and L&D
Initiated a DEI pilot with an {ld|Inclusive Leadership workshop} for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
Facilitated smooth {od|organizational change} by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing {cross|cross-functional stakeholders}
Optimized onboarding processes across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Strengthened {ex|engagement} via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to {perf|performance management}
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Group Dynamics & Leadership, Consumer Psychology

education.degree: BA in Psychology and Education Studies (GPA: 4.00)
bullets: Relevant Coursework: Educational Psychology, Practicum in Education Studies, Psychological Testing, Learning and Motivation

projects: Consulting Projects: Enhanced Elanco Animal Health employer branding & talent attraction strategy via survey design/execution
Master's Capstone: Examined non-monetary factors shaping employer attractiveness for YouTube via mixed-method research
Scholarships: Freeman Asian Scholarship (4-year full-tuition), Tuck Bridge Scholarship (full)
skills: Org Development: Organizational Development · Change Management · Talent Strategy · Performance Management · Leadership Development
Data & PM: Data Analysis (Excel, Google Sheets, SPSS) · Project Management (Asana, Airtable) · LMS (Canvas, Moodle) · MS Office
Languages & Certs: English (fluent) · Mandarin (native) · Spanish (basic) · HRCI HR Associate Certificate (in progress)

jd.meta: Valley Medical Center (UW Medicine) — Talent & Organizational Development Partner, Renton WA, cap-exempt hospital, $86.3-129.4K. Scope: OD strategy, employee engagement (surveys/action planning), leadership development, performance management (annual eval design), onboarding/orientation facilitation, externship/pipeline partnerships with educational institutions. Reports to Dir Employee & Labor Relations. Reqs: BA + 5yr HR/OD/Talent (Jamie ~3yr = STRETCH); LMS preferred; healthcare preferred not required.

changeLog: 2026-06-12: Tailored from base for Valley Medical Talent & OD Partner (cap-exempt hospital, Renton WA). Recipe: Talent Management / OD. Summary identity = Organization & Talent Development professional (3 pillars intact). InGenius title = Program Enablement Manager. ODN = 3 bullets, BOTH projects kept separate (Pair A quantified $362K leave-cost result + Project 2 NGO leadership/accountability diagnosis) — matches JD's OD-diagnosis + data-to-recommendations scope. Bullet emphasis surfaces: leadership development (Vestas Inclusive Leadership, NextGen manager effectiveness), employee engagement (NextGen 2,000+ analysis, survey/rewards), performance management (NextGen perf review consult, Kronos perf mgmt), onboarding facilitation (InGenius + Vestas). Added an OD-skills line (Org Development/Change Mgmt/Talent Strategy/Performance Mgmt/Leadership Dev) — all truthful to her OD work; LMS listed (JD-preferred). Header = Renton WA (role city) + Open to Relocation. HONEST GAP: JD asks 5yr min; Jamie has ~3 + MS — applied as STRETCH per recalibrated filter + cap-exempt context. No fabricated claims; all bullets sourced to content_library.md.
